description_long: This is a small, simple cabin with a rectangular footprint and a steeply pitched gable roof. The exterior walls are plain and light-colored, likely finished in painted wood or siding, while the roof is a darker, textured material, possibly shingles or metal. The structure sits isolated on a flat, circular base, suggesting it is meant to be viewed as a standalone building. There are no visible windows or decorative elements, giving the cabin a minimalist and utilitarian appearance. The only architectural feature visible is a single door on one of the shorter facades, which is centered and unadorned. The overall design is compact and functional, suitable for a small residential or utility building in an urban or suburban context.
description: A small, minimalist cabin with a steep gable roof, light walls, and a single centered door. The design is simple and functional, with no visible windows or decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the centered door on the short facade and the steep gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the plain wall and the full slope of the gable roof, with no windows visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a blank wall with no openings or features, and the roof's ridge.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing a plain wall and the roof's pitch, with the door not visible.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with a steep pitch, covered in dark shingles or metal
attribute_window_arrangement: No windows are visible on any side of the cabin
attribute_door_placement: A single exterior door is centered on the front facade
